summary: Six psalms of individual lament and trust — several connected to David's flight from Absalom — moving from desperate 'how long' cries to confident rest in God's sustaining care.
psalm_rows_html:
PsalmTypeAuthorSituationKey ThemeNotable VerseNT Connection
3Individual LamentDavidFleeing from his son AbsalomGod as shield, glory, and lifter of the head; trust and sleep amid surrounding enemies"But you, Lord, are a shield around me, my glory, the One who lifts my head high." (v.3)Christ's vindication through resurrection; the Father who does not abandon his Son even in death
4Individual Lament / TrustDavidEvening prayer amid political adversaries; likely the Absalom crisisSecurity and peace found in God alone, not in prosperity; anger without sin"In peace I will lie down and sleep, for you alone, Lord, make me dwell in safety." (v.8)Eph 4:26 quotes v.4 ("In your anger do not sin"); Christ as our true peace and Sabbath rest
12Community LamentDavidMoral collapse of society; faithlessness and flattery rampantGod's flawless word contrasted with the corrupt speech of the wicked; protection of the poor"The words of the Lord are flawless, like silver purified in a crucible, like gold refined seven times." (v.6)James 3 on the destructive tongue; Jesus as the trustworthy Word of God (John 1)
13Individual LamentDavidExtended suffering with no apparent relief; enemies gaining the upper handThe classic lament arc: fourfold "How long?" moving to petitions, then to confident trust and praise"But I trust in your unfailing love; my heart rejoices in your salvation." (v.5)Christ's cry of dereliction (Matt 27:46); the pattern of lament-to-praise fulfilled in resurrection
28Individual Lament / PraiseDavidThreat from deceitful enemies who speak peace but harbor maliceGod as strength and shield; prayer heard and turned immediately to doxology"The Lord is my strength and my shield; my heart trusts in him, and he helps me." (v.7)Christ our great intercessor; the Lord who will not remain silent for his people (cf. Heb 7:25)
55Individual Lament (Maskil)DavidBetrayal by a close companion; often linked to Ahithophel during Absalom's revoltThe wound of intimate betrayal; casting all burdens on God who sustains and never lets the righteous be shaken"Cast your cares on the Lord and he will sustain you; he will never let the righteous be shaken." (v.22)Judas' betrayal of Christ; 1 Peter 5:7 echoes v.22; Christ forsaken yet entrusting himself to the Father
